pi_items.source: Task description specifies this exact path as the save location for the screenshot
DreamPlan tutorial (https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/tutorial.html) confirms 'brick' is listed as an available wall texture option in 'Painting Walls and Selecting Flooring' video tutorial
Path follows standard Windows AppData conventions for per-user application data. Setup scripts reference this structure implicitly through NCH Software's standard installation patterns
Official website https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/index.html and setup scripts confirm DreamPlan is published by NCH Software

Task setup_dreamplan.ps1 script (lines 233-236) explicitly calls Launch-DreamPlanWithSample function and task_utils.ps1 (lines 489-550) shows Open-ContemporaryHouseFromStartScreen function that navigates to and opens 'Contemporary House' sample project

pi_items.verified_value: DreamPlan sample projects downloaded from NCH Software servers
pi_items.source: Setup scripts show DreamPlan downloads sample projects on first launch (task_utils.ps1 line 512 mentions 'First-time: samples download takes ~30-45s'). install_dreamplan.ps1 line 99-106 shows data staging from workspace/data if available, otherwise samples come from DreamPlan itself

privileged_info_summary: The change_exterior_siding task uses DreamPlan Home Design Software by NCH Software with the Contemporary House sample project. The task requires changing exterior ground-floor walls from default siding to Brick material. The output screenshot should be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\exterior_brick_result.png. Project data comes from DreamPlan's built-in sample projects (Contemporary House), which is real data downloaded from NCH Software's servers, not synthetic. The software supports brick as an exterior wall material option as documented in the official tutorial.
